skill.name: gpt-image-2
skill.description: Generate images with GPT Image 2 (ChatGPT Images 2.0) inside Claude Code, using your existing ChatGPT Plus or Pro subscription — no separate OpenAI access, no per-image billing. Supports text-to-image, image-to-image editing, style transfer, and multi-reference composition via the local Codex CLI. Triggers on "gpt image 2", "gpt-image-2", "ChatGPT Images 2.0", "image 2", or any explicit ask to generate or edit an image through the user's ChatGPT plan.
skill.summary: Generate images with GPT Image 2 inside Claude Code using your ChatGPT Plus or Pro subscription via the local Codex CLI
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All static findings are legitimate CLI integration patterns. External commands call codex exec properly; filesystem access is limited to ~/.codex/sessions/ and temp files for session diffing. No malicious code detected. Risk factors are expected for CLI-based skills.

low_findings.title: CLI Command Execution
low_findings.description: The script invokes 'codex exec' command to generate images. This is the legitimate and intended method for Codex CLI integration. Arguments are hardcoded arrays, not user-injected strings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Codex exec is the documented API for using the imagegen tool. The instruction string is constructed from the --prompt argument which comes from explicit user input in Claude, a legitimate use case.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Access to Codex Sessions
low_findings.description: The script reads from ~/.codex/sessions/ to extract generated images. Access is scoped to only new files created during the run via directory diffing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Session directory access is the only way to retrieve images from codex exec output. The diffing approach ensures only new files from this run are accessed.

low_findings.title: Temp File Creation for Session Tracking
low_findings.description: The script creates temporary files to track before/after session states for diffing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard shell scripting pattern for tracking directory changes. Files are properly cleaned up via EXIT trap.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Flag (False Positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at multiple locations. Analysis shows no cryptographic operations exist in the code. Base64.b64decode() is encoding/decoding, not encryption. This is a false positive from the scanner.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic operations found. The base64 usage is for image data encoding which is standard for API responses.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden File Access (Expected Behavior)
dangerous_patterns.description: Access to ~/.codex/ directory is intentional and documented. This is where Codex stores session data and is the required location for extracting generated images.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Hidden file access is documented in SKILL.md and is the only way to retrieve image data from Codex CLI.

dangerous_patterns.title: Python Base64 Decode (Legitimate Usage)
dangerous_patterns.description: Base64 decoding at extract_image.py:111 is the correct method to decode the PNG/JPEG/WebP image data embedded in Codex session rollouts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Image data from the imagegen tool is returned as base64 in the session JSONL. Decoding is the only way to write the actual image file.

content.user_title: Generate images with GPT Image 2 in Claude Code
content.value_statement: Claude Code agents can now generate images using GPT Image 2 without separate API setup — using your existing ChatGPT Plus or Pro subscription via the local Codex CLI. Text-to-image, image-to-image editing, and style transfer are all supported.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using GPT Image 2 through your ChatGPT Plus or Pro subscription
Edit existing images using image-to-image reference mode with style transfer
Use multiple reference images for multi-reference composition
Extract and save generated images as PNG, JPEG, or WebP files
Integrate image generation into Claude Code agent workflows with proper error handling
limitations: Requires ChatGPT Plus or Pro subscription with image generation entitlement
Requires local installation and login of the Codex CLI
Image generation quota depends on your ChatGPT plan limits

use_cases.title: Generate product images for e-commerce
use_cases.description: Create product mockups, lifestyle shots, or variations for online stores using natural language descriptions. Perfect for catalog generation and A/B testing visual assets.

use_cases.title: Create illustrations for documentation
use_cases.description: Generate diagrams, flowcharts, and visual examples for technical documentation. Describe the concept in plain language and receive matching illustrations.

use_cases.title: Iterative image editing with reference images
use_cases.description: Upload a reference image and ask for style changes, color adjustments, or composition modifications. The model preserves structure while applying your edits.

prompt_templates.title: Basic text-to-image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT Image 2 to generate an image of [describe your image concept]
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants a simple image from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-image with style reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT Image 2 to repaint [reference image path] in [style description] style
prompt_templates.scenario: When user has a reference image and wants style transfer

prompt_templates.title: Multi-reference composition
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT Image 2 with --ref [image1] --ref [image2] to generate [describe the result]
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to combine elements from multiple reference images

prompt_templates.title: Custom output path with timeout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT Image 2 with --out [path] --timeout-sec [seconds] to generate [describe image]
prompt_templates.scenario: When user specifies a custom output location or needs longer timeout

output_examples.input: Use GPT Image 2 to generate a flat-color icon of a coffee cup on a table in watercolor style
output_examples.output: An image file is created at the specified path (default: ./image-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.png) containing a watercolor-style illustration of a coffee cup on a table with soft brush strokes and light blue tones.

output_examples.input: Use GPT Image 2 to repaint this logo in ukiyo-e woodblock print style
output_examples.output: The original logo composition is preserved while the rendering style changes to traditional Japanese woodblock print with period-appropriate colors, bold outlines, and characteristic texture.

best_practices: Be specific in your image descriptions — include subject, setting, style, mood, and any specific elements needed
Use image-to-image (--ref) when you have a reference image to preserve structure while changing style
Test different style keywords if the first attempt does not match your vision — style transfer can be iterative
anti_patterns: Do not use GPT Image 2 for plain image generation requests if the user did not specify this tool — ask for confirmation first
Do not substitute DALL-E, Midjourney, or HTML mockups without user permission when GPT Image 2 was requested
Do not rewrite or translate the user's prompt unless explicitly asked — pass their intent through as-is

faq.question: What subscription do I need for this skill?
faq.answer: You need ChatGPT Plus or Pro with image generation capability enabled. The skill uses your existing ChatGPT session through the Codex CLI.

faq.question: How do I install the Codex CLI?
faq.answer: Run 'brew install codex' on macOS or see github.com/openai/codex for other platforms. Then run 'codex login' to authenticate with your ChatGPT account.

faq.question: Does this skill add per-image costs?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses your existing ChatGPT subscription. No additional OpenAI API billing or third-party image service charges apply.

faq.question: Can I use reference images for style transfer?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --ref flag multiple times to pass reference images. The model preserves composition from your references while applying style changes.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: Input prompts generate PNG output by default. You can also request JPEG or WebP by specifying the output path with the appropriate extension.

faq.question: Why did image generation fail?
faq.answer: Check your exit code: 3 means codex or python3 is missing, 5 means codex exec failed (auth or network issue), 7 means imagegen did not produce output (quota or capability not enabled).
